spec.subject: a motorcycle engine crankcase cover in polished chrome

lighting.key: warm soft-box from camera-left, 5500K, high intensity with slight diffusion to catch chrome highlights
lighting.fill: cool large-area panel from camera-right, 7500K, 40% of key intensity to preserve contrast in reflections
lighting.rim: narrow strip light from directly behind subject, 6000K, separating chrome edge from dark background

palette.dominant: mirror chrome silver and near-white specular highlights
palette.neutral: soft warm grey mid-reflection zone on chrome surface

composition.foreground: subtle dark surface plane casting a faint chrome reflection of the cover
composition.midground: the crankcase cover centered and slightly left of frame center
composition.background: deep neutral dark gradient, no distracting elements
composition.hero: the polished chrome crankcase cover surface catching the key light specular

materials.notes: roughness ~0.02, metallic 1.0, high reflectivity, minor micro-scratches via normal map to break up perfect mirror and read as real machined metal; no clearcoat layer needed
roughness ~0.3, dark charcoal color to create subtle reflection of the cover without competing with subject

density.scattered: none — isolated product shot, no scattered elements

spec.scale_notes: Motorcycle crankcase cover approximately 0.25m wide x 0.20m tall x 0.08m deep. Camera placed roughly 0.6m from subject to fill frame with 85mm lens. Scene footprint reads at under 1m across — tight product scale must be respected to avoid lighting that reads as architectural.
